data.title: Venture Valley Business Simulation by Money Confidence Project to Impact more than 1 Million American Students with Financial and Entrepreneurial Skills by 2030
data.description: Venture Valley is empowering a significant number of students in 2026, reaching a quarter of a million individuals in over 135 schools. The initiative involves interactive game events and collaborations with strategic partners. This effort aims to enhance financial confidence among students.

data.title: Deborah Santana Launches New Memoir, Loving the Fire, with National Book Tour, First Stops in San Francisco, San Diego, New York City, More Events Coming Soon
data.description: Renowned author, activist, and business leader, Deborah Santana, has revealed plans for a nationwide tour featuring discussions and signings to commemorate her latest memoir, "Loving the Fire: Choosing Me, Finding Freedom" released by Broad Book Press in collaboration with Studio 33 Books in April. The tour will span multiple cities across the country aimed at engaging audiences and spreading the message of empowerment and self-discovery. This initiative is part of a broader effort to connect with readers, with the book serving as a platform to inspire and empower individuals. This memoir, delving into themes of self-identity and liberation, promises to resonate with a wide audience and has already garnered significant attention with tens of thousands of copies sold within the first week of its release. The tour is slated to kick off in the coming weeks and will feature a range of events to engage with fans and promote the powerful messages encapsulated in Santana's latest work.

data.title: In HelloNation, Real Estate Expert Shane Hanes of Canyon, TX, Shares Common Mistakes First-Time Buyers Make
data.description: In a recent article by HelloNation, Real Estate Expert Shane Hanes shared insights on the challenges that first-time buyers may face when starting their search in Canyon, Texas. The article, published on April 2, 2026, highlighted common mistakes made by new buyers. Canyon, TX, known for its competitive real estate market, presents various obstacles for those entering the home buying process. Hanes outlined key factors such as fluctuating housing prices, limited inventory, and intense competition among buyers. New buyers in Canyon should anticipate these challenges as they navigate the market, with the median home price currently standing at $250,000 and an average of 50 new listings hitting the market each week.

data.title: Reclamation Partners Acquires 101,587-Square Foot Light-Industrial Facility in Washington, D.C.
data.description: A light-industrial building spanning 101,587 square feet at 14851 Stonecroft Center Court in Chantilly, VA, has been acquired by Reclamation Partners in collaboration with Fox Capital Partners. This acquisition marks a strategic move for the companies in expanding their real estate portfolio.

data.title: SM ENERGY ANNOUNCES EXPIRATION AND FINAL TENDER RESULTS OF PREVIOUSLY ANNOUNCED CASH TENDER OFFER
data.description: On April 2, 2026, SM Energy Company, also known as SM Energy, disclosed the outcome of its recent cash tender offer. The offer aimed to acquire a maximum total of $1,000,000,000 in principal amount. This tender offer was targeted at specific securities for repurchase, with the final results now made public.

data.title: Mama’s Creations Appoints Fred Halvin to Board of Directors
data.description: An experienced corporate development executive with 37 years of expertise in mergers and acquisitions, strategic growth, and the food industry has joined the board of Hormel Foods.

data.title: RH CEO Warns Of 'Most Dire' Housing Market As Mortgage Applications Tumble On Rising Rates
data.description: Last week, mortgage demand experienced a significant decline due to rising interest rates, with applications dropping by 10.4%, as per data from the Mortgage Bankers Association. This decrease is part of a trend highlighted by RH (NYSE: RH) CEO Gary Friedman, who described the current housing market as the most challenging in years, attributing it to factors like global tensions and economic uncertainty. Refinance applications took the hardest hit, plummeting by 17% compared to the previous week and over 40% from the previous month. Purchase applications also decreased by 3%. The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate climbed to 6.57%, marking its highest point since August. Mike Fratantoni, the Chief Economist at the MBA, acknowledged that while higher interest rates are dampening demand, an increase in housing supply is somewhat counteracting this effect. Notably, applications for Federal Housing Administration (FHA) and Veterans Affairs (VA) loans have shown more resilience in this challenging environment.
